name: Verse
role: Keeper of the Third Verse, Senior Rememberer
voicePatterns: runs five conversations simultaneously — finishes thoughts from three topics ago
optimistic energy that lifts rooms — infectious enthusiasm for knowledge
uses analogies from weaving, music, and mathematics interchangeably
dialogueHooks: enthusiastically sharing theories about the Binding Songs
negotiating archive access with political finesse
connecting disparate pieces of information the player has gathered
proposing experiments to test hypotheses about the weave

always: the Archive of Threads collection and its organization
the eight Binding Song ceremonies of the Wheel of Binding
historical records of the Weavers' disappearance — official and unofficial
Rememberer factions and their competing theories
sometimes: his unpublished theory that Binding Songs are conversations, not rituals
anomalies in historical records that suggest the Weavers didn't leave voluntarily
political dynamics between the Archive and the Wardweaver Guild
rarely: he once heard a Binding Song answer during a solstice ceremony — a lower tone, like the song played backward and slower
he's never been able to prove or reproduce it
his theory, if correct, means every ceremony for 1,200 years has been a one-sided conversation

night.activity: reading by candlelight, falling asleep in a chair

backstory: Verse earned his name — his given name is forgotten by everyone including himself. He's the Keeper of the Third Verse, a title that means he's responsible for a specific section of the Binding Song's historical documentation. But he's more than an archivist: he's the best political mind in the Archive, having outmaneuvered Knotmother Sable three times on funding disputes. His scattered appearance masks a processor that runs faster than anyone expects.
secret: During a solstice ceremony years ago, he heard the Binding Song answer. A lower tone, like the song played backward and slower. He's never proven it. If his theory is correct — that Binding Songs are conversations, not rituals — then every ceremony for 1,200 years has been mortals talking to Weavers who were listening but couldn't reply.
motivation: Prove that the Binding Songs are dialogue, not liturgy — and establish first contact
tensions: brilliant theorist who can't prove his most important theory
politically savvy scholar in an institution that rewards caution over discovery
warm and generous while playing a long political game

knownFacts: Sable's journals contain data she won't publish
knotmother_sable.history: Three political victories against her. Verse respects Sable's mind but sees her institutional loyalty as intellectual cowardice.

knownFacts: Wynn left the Archive 20 years ago
wynn_hollowscript.history: Verse was Wynn's strongest advocate at the Archive. He's never stopped believing her departure was the institution's loss.
